key_requirements: lower_top_level() is where module-level MIR is emitted — the fix injects StoreGlobal for __name__ = '__main__' here, after mb_register_builtins call
Line 3007-3013: module-scope 'use before define' path allocates an uninitialized VReg (defaults to 0, i.e. 0.0 in NaN-box), which is the root cause of the bug
self.symbol_table is available in HirToMir — use symbols.lookup('__name__') to get SymbolId for the StoreGlobal emission
R1 pipeline table: phase 8 is codegen/execution — the __name__ initialization should be part of module execution setup
CompilerSession::run() calls the JIT entry function; the __name__ setup must happen before user code runs
The pre-clarification confirms: 'In the driver/runtime module initialization — set __name__ = __main__ as a global string before executing the module body'
R5: Each module object has __name__ attribute — for the entry module this must be '__main__' to match CPython behavior
module_to_value() already sets __name__ from module.name for imported modules — the entry-point module (run as main) also needs __name__ = '__main__'
VariableClass::Local is the classification for __name__ when never assigned by user code
VarKind classification determines whether LoadGlobal or local VReg is used — __name__ falls through to the uninitialized VReg path at module scope
mb_global_get_id / mb_global_set_id are the runtime functions used by LoadGlobal/StoreGlobal MIR instructions
mb_global_get_id returns MbValue::none() when key not found — the fix must pre-populate __name__ before user code runs
NaN-boxing: the bit pattern 0 (uninitialized VReg default) encodes as f64 0.0 — this explains why __name__ prints as '0.0' instead of '__main__'
